DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF MARINA PITTS' MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS MOOT AS THE POLICY HAS BEEN PRODUCED SINCE THE FILING OF THE MOTION. THE COURT FINDS THE LATE PRODUCTION WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $850, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. LATE OPPOSITION HAS BEEN CONSIDERED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
H. PAUL EFSTRATIS, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Rinda Burleigh & Lewis Burleigh
No Other appearances.

TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendants Rinda Burleigh, Lewis Burleigh's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to Document Request Numbers 1, 20, 21, and 22 as Plaintiff shall respond further as set forth in the opposition.
The motion is denied as to Document Request Numbers 18, 19, and 23 as the requests are irrelevant.
The motion is granted as to Request For Admission Numbers 10, 13, 16, 17, and 18.
The motion is denied as to Request For Admission Numbers 11, 12, 14, and 15 as the requests are irrelevant.
The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatories 17.1 as to Request for Admission Numebrs 10, 13, 16, 17, and 18.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of P/A In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Gregory E. Stubbs In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
H. PAUL EFSTRATIS, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Rinda Burleigh & Lewis Burleigh
No Other appearances.

TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Marina Pitts' Motion For Protective Order is ordered off calendar. A hearsay proof of personal service is not competent evidence that the motion was served. The proof of personal service must be signed by the person who actually served the motion. However, the motion is moot as Plaintiff has withdrawn one of the experts. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Inspection Of Deft'S Premises And Req. For Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Marina Pitts' Motion To Compel Inspection Of Defendants' Premises is granted, limited as follows: the scope shall be as set forth in the scope of the demand served by Plaintiff. Additionally, there will be no destructive testing permitted, and photographs and video taping shall be limited to what is necessary to assist in any diagraming of the structure and to exclude photography of personal possessions, except as they would naturally be included in necessary structural representations. All requests for sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Gregory Stubbs, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Marina Pitts

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Marina Pitt's Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendants' Financial Information is denied. Off Calendar Jul-31-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Gregory Stubbs, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Marina Pitts; Joshua Rosen, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Rinda Burleigh & Lewis Burleigh

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff Marina Pitts' Motion To Compel Production Of Documents is moot as the policy has been produced since the filing of the motion. The court finds the late production was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $850, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Late opposition has been considered. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
